# Shared constraints for the .venv-train install (pip `-c`), used by BOTH the CI workflow and
# scripts/setup_training.sh. requirements-train.txt is intentionally unpinned; these are the exact
# versions.
#
# The train stack has a real tension: stable-baselines3 (pulled by godot-rl 0.8.2) caps numpy<2.0,
# but the onnx/ml_dtypes chain wants numpy>=2. This set is the lowest common denominator that
# installs AND imports on every Python we use — local 3.13 and CI 3.12 (all have matching wheels,
# so nothing builds from source):
#   - numpy==1.26.4  : newest numpy<2 (satisfies SB3 2.4.0).
#   - ml-dtypes==0.4.1: last ml_dtypes wheel for numpy<2. Without it pip backtracks to the ml_dtypes
#                       0.4.0 *sdist*, whose build-time numpy==2.0.0rc1 doesn't exist → install crash.
#                       (ml_dtypes>=0.5 needs numpy>=2.1 on py3.13, so it is NOT an option locally.)
#   - onnx==1.17.0   : onnx>=1.18 references ml_dtypes.float4_e2m1fn at *import* time (only in
#                       ml_dtypes>=0.5 / numpy>=2). So onnx 1.19 RESOLVES under numpy<2 but crashes on
#                       `import onnx`, killing every torch.onnx.export. 1.17.0 imports under 0.4.x.
#   - onnx-ir==0.1.8, onnxscript==0.5.0: the dynamo-exporter chain compatible with onnx 1.17.0.
#
# To move to numpy>=2 / newer onnx you must first move off godot-rl 0.8.2 (it pins SB3<=2.4.0).
# The convert venv (.venv, py3.14) is installed WITHOUT these constraints (no sb3/ml_dtypes there;
# numpy resolves to 2.x cleanly). See docs/dev/gotchas.md.
numpy==1.26.4
ml-dtypes==0.4.1
onnx==1.17.0
onnx-ir==0.1.8
onnxscript==0.5.0
